results.title: Staff Machine Learning Engineer, Cisco
highlights:
Staff Machine Learning Engineer, Cisco
[...]
# Staff Machine Learning Engineer, Cisco
[...]
Cisco is a global technology leader advancing innovation across AI, data, and cloud infrastructure. The Staff Machine Learning Engineer role focuses on building scalable, production-ready AI and Generative AI systems that support intelligent products and agent-driven workflows. This position plays a key role in turning research into reliable solutions while shaping architecture, standards, and cross-team innovation.
[...]
Location: Hyderabad, Telangana, India
[...]
Job Type: Professional
[...]
### Primary Responsibilities
[...]
Partner with engineering, product, and data science teams to translate requirements into scalable AI/ML solutions.
[...]
Build, deploy, and scale AI/ML and Generative AI services from experimentation through full production.
[...]
Design and operate high-performance AI systems optimized for latency, throughput, scalability, and cost efficiency.
[...]
Drive adoption of agentic AI architectures, LLM-powered features, and modern ML platform capabilities.
[...]
Identify innovation opportunities by exploring emerging AI/ML approaches and converting them into technical proposals.
[...]
Stay current with advancements in AI/ML and apply relevant innovations to active initiatives.
[...]
Influence system architecture and guide engineering best practices for ML platforms and cloud-native AI systems.
[...]
### Minimum Qualifications
[...]
Strong backend development skills which should be demonstrated through their Python programming abilities or through their Java programming abilities.
[...]
Develop services that operate through API and event-driven methods which handle AI inference processes and agent orchestration and data pipelines.
[...]
Experience in working with Generative AI technology and Large Language Models.
[...]
Knowledge of agent-based AI systems which use LangGraph orchestration frameworks for their operations.
[...]
Extensive knowledge of Kubernetes and container technology which includes experience using these tools for machine learning training and inference testing on both CPU and GPU platforms.
[...]
Expertise in DevOps/MLOps systems together with CI/CD pipeline development for model training and evaluation and promotion processes.
[...]
Experience in using Git and Jenkins and ArgoCD together with other CI/CD tools.
[...]
Possesses practical experience working with cloud platforms which include AWS and GCP and Azure.
[...]
Expertise in designing and managing AI and machine learning systems which achieve high-performance standards.
[...]
### Preferred Qualifications
[...]
Demonstrate proven technical leadership abilities which include architectural design and engineer mentoring skills.
[...]
Employ systems-thinking abilities to achieve a balance between model quality, system scalability, system reliability, system latency and system operational costs.
[...]
Capacity to implement AI and machine learning methods in operational production environments.
[...]
Maintain a strong dedication to staying updated on current developments in artificial intelligence and machine learning and Generative AI technologies.
[...]
### About Cisco
[...]
Cisco is transforming the connections between data and infrastructure and artificial intelligence to create new digital solutions. Through more than 40 years of innovative research Cisco develops secure intelligent systems that operate across both physical and digital environments. Our organizational culture depends on teamwork and understanding others and ongoing education and innovative thinking which enables our staff members to develop solutions that affect the entire world.

# AI/ML Engineer (Hybrid)
YesterdayDurham, North CarolinaSan Jose, CaliforniahybridSeniorStaffML / AIData EngineeringBackend
[...]
$137k – $278k
[...]
Senior ML engineer building AI agents and predictive models to optimize Cisco's multi-cloud infrastructure and operations across AWS, GCP, and Azure.
[...]
, North Carolina
[...]
Cisco Cloudx team is a dynamic group of developers, Solutions Architects, and data engineers dedicated to supporting Cisco’s public cloud infrastructure and operations. As part of the Cloud Management and Operations (CMO) team within Cisco IT, we build the platforms and automations that enable governance, operational efficiency, cost management, and security guardrails across Cisco’s public cloud environments.
[...]
As a Software Engineer specialized in AI/ML, you will guide the architecture, design, and development of our Cloud platforms and automations ensuring high standards of security, performance, and scalability. You’ll champion technology innovation, mentor your development team, and collaborate with stakeholders to deliver business-critical features that shape the future of Cisco's public cloud journey. You will:
[...]
* Design and implement AI Agents to monitor and optimize cloud resources based on findings and recommendations from Cloud Service Providers.
* Integrate AI-driven insights with Cloud Service Providers like AWS, GCP, Azure, and existing data and tools.
[...]
* Develop predictive models for drift detection, cost anomaly detection, and forecasting of public cloud resources and spend.
* Automate operational workflows using machine learning and intelligent scripting.
[...]
* Design, build, and maintain scalable ETL pipelines using AWS Glue and other cloud-native services.
[...]
* Use AWS
[...]
* Orchestrate complex workflows and data pipelines using Apache Airflow DAGs.
[...]
* Continuously evaluate emerging AI technologies and tools for operational improvements.
[...]
* Maintain documentation and standard processes for AI/ML integration in cloud systems.# **Minimum Qualifications **
[...]
* 7+ years of experience in Computer Science along with Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Computer Science, Data Science, or related technical field.
[...]
* At least 2 years of experience in building and deploying ML models focused on cloud operations.
[...]
* Solid knowledge of cloud technologies (AWS, GCP, Azure, OCI).
[...]
* Experience with Python, PySpark, and ML libraries such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, or Scikit-Learn.
[...]
* Experience with AWS Glue ETL, AWS Athena, Snowflake, and Apache Airflow DAGs.# **Preferred Qualifications **
[...]
* Full stack development experience with Angular for front end and Flask for back end application development to build platforms to support our Stakeholders and Business Units within Cisco
[...]
* Comfortable working with streaming data, APIs, and telemetry systems.
[...]
* Experience with Agile and DevOps operating models, including project tracking tools (e.g., Jira), Git (any Version Control systems), and CI/CD systems (e.g., GitLab, GitHub Actions, Jenkins).# Why Cisco? 
At Cisco, we’re revolutionizing how data and infrastructure connect and protect organizations in the AI era – and beyond. We’ve been innovating fearlessly for 40 years to create solutions that power how humans and technology work together across the physical and digital worlds. These solutions provide customers with unparalleled security, visibility, and insights across the entire digital footprint.
[...]
The starting salary range posted for this position is $137,000.00 to $200,500.00 and reflects the projected salary range for new hires in this position in U.S. and/or Canada locations, not including incentive compensation\*, equity, or benefits.
[...]
### Staff Machine Learning Engineer (Remote)

results.title: AI Data scientist job in San Jose, CA, United States at Largeton Group
highlights:
AI Data scientist job in San Jose, CA, United States at Largeton Group

# AI Data scientist

Largeton Group5 days ago

San Jose, CA, United States

On-site

Contract

Junior Level (1-3 years)

## Job Description

## Job Summary: AI Data Scientist (Cisco, San Jose, CA)

- Hands-on expertise with Generative AI (Gen AI) and Large Language Models (LLMs), including tools such as Langchain, Langgraph, RAG, RAGAS, LangSmith, DeepEval, Semantic Search, Reranking, Vector Databases, Prompt Engineering, and LLM toolcall.
- Strong background in Natural Language Processing (NLP) with experience in BERT, SBERT, Transformers, word2vec, Glove, FastText, RNNs, LSTMs, and DIET Classifier (RASA).
- Solid experience in designing and architecting standalone systems, with at least 8+ years of end-to-end system implementation.
- Proficiency in machine learning algorithms and models such as XGBoost, Random Forest, GBM, Neural Networks, Logistic Regression, and Time Series Models.
- Engineering and deployment experience with Django, REST APIs, FAST APIs, Websockets, Microservices, Kubernetes, Helm, and Git.
- Cloud and database knowledge, particularly AWS (DynamoDB, OpenSearch) and Vector Databases.
- Experience in building and deploying APIs and microservices.
- Minimum of 10+ years of relevant professional experience required.
- Onsite role in San Jose, CA (long-term contract).

## Required Skills

word2vec

SBERT

Websockets

FAST APIs

Time Series Models

results.title: Full Stack Engineer, AI/ML, Partner Incentives, Cisco
highlights:
Full Stack Engineer, AI/ML, Partner Incentives, Cisco
[...]
# Full Stack Engineer, AI/ML, Partner Incentives, Cisco
[...]
Cisco is seeking to employ a Full Stack Engineer who specializes in AI and ML work on Partner Incentives projects for its office in Hyderabad. The hybrid position requires employees to develop AI-powered intelligent solutions that improve Cisco partner incentive programs through their skills in full-stack development and machine learning and generative AI technology. The global technology leader develops innovative solutions that combine data processing with artificial intelligence and enterprise platform systems.
[...]
### Primary Responsibilities
[...]
Design and build scalable AI/ML applications for partner incentive solutions.
[...]
Develop and optimize predictive models using advanced machine learning techniques.
[...]
Translate business needs into data-driven analytical and engineering solutions.
[...]
Integrate and fine-tune LLMs and NLP models for enterprise use cases.
[...]
Manage MLOps pipelines, ensure data quality, and collaborate across teams.
[...]
### Key Technologies & Skills
[...]
Programming Languages: Python, SQL
[...]
Machine Learning: Regression, classification, clustering, supervised and unsupervised learning
[...]
AI & LLMs: Generative AI, NLP, RAG, fine-tuning, agentic AI frameworks
[...]
Frameworks & Libraries: TensorFlow, PyTorch, scikit-learn, Pandas, NumPy
[...]
Full Stack & APIs: ReactJS, FastAPI
[...]
Databases & Search: Snowflake, Oracle, ElasticSearch, Apache Solr
[...]
Streaming & Messaging: Kafka
[...]
MLOps: CI/CD pipelines, model monitoring, deployment automation
[...]
### Minimum Qualifications
[...]
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, AI/ML, or a related field.
[...]
Experience with more than one year in AI/ML developing while also performing full-stack engineering duties.
[...]
Exceptional skills in Python programming together with expertise in data science libraries.
[...]
Practical experience in working with traditional machine learning algorithms and contemporary large language models.
[...]
Experience in constructing and operating MLOps pipelines.
[...]
Knowledge about creating and implementing natural language processing models.
[...]
### Preferred Qualifications
[...]
Experience in developing machine learning models through their work on LLM-based solutions which include RAG and fine-tuning.
[...]
Have knowledge about unit testing and integration testing.
[...]
Hands-on experience with ElasticSearch, Kafka, FastAPI and ReactJS.
[...]
Worked with both Oracle and Snowflake database systems.
[...]
### About Cisco
[...]
Cisco operates as a worldwide technological leader which provides internet support while developing artificial intelligence-based digital transformation technologies. Organizations across the world use Cisco's networking and cloud and security and data platform innovations to establish secure connections and develop new ideas. The Cisco workplace environment promotes teamwork and diversity and continuous education according to its workers who create worldwide effects through their work.
[...]
###### Staff Machine Learning Engineer, Cisco

# Senior Kubernetes Platform Engineer - AI Infrastructure
3 weeks agoDurham, North CarolinaDallas, TexasAllen, TexashybridSeniorDevOps & InfraML / AI
[...]
LLMTelemetryIaCIncident ResponseGPUMetrics
[...]
Senior platform engineer designing and operating large-scale on-prem Kubernetes infrastructure for AI/ML workloads, including GPU-enabled environments and LLM deployment pipelines at Cisco.
[...]
Senior Kubernetes Platform Engineer - AI Infrastructure - hybrid (2013580)
[...]
Join our Platform Engineering team to design, build, and operate large-scale, on-prem Kubernetes infrastructure powering next-generation AI/ML platforms, including GPU-enabled environments for both traditional ML and state-of-the-art LLM workloads.
[...]
You will be pivotal in defining and evolving a highly scalable Kubernetes platform that serves as the foundation for AI/ML workloads. This role combines deep Kubernetes platform engineering with AI/ML infrastructure enablement, ensuring performance, reliability, and scalability across distributed systems.
[...]
You will lead technical direction across Kubernetes control plane operations, cluster lifecycle management, and platform extensibility, while working closely with data scientists, ML engineers, and infrastructure teams to support production AI workloads at scale.
[...]
* Architect, build, and operate large-scale on-prem Kubernetes platforms (OpenShift/Anthos), including control plane and etcd lifecycle management
[...]
* Define and evolve scalable, multi-tenant platform architecture supporting AI/ML and GPU-based workloads
* Enable and optimize ML workloads including training, inference, and LLM deployment pipelines on Kubernetes
[...]
* Build platform extensions using Kubernetes controllers, operators, CRDs, and Golang-based services
[...]
* Implement Infrastructure as Code and automation to improve scalability, consistency, and operational efficiency
[...]
* Drive AIOps capabilities using telemetry, automation, anomaly detection, and self-healing systems for platform reliability
[...]
* Improve observability (metrics, logs, traces) and optimize resource utilization, scheduling, and cluster performance
[...]
* Partner with ML engineers and data scientists to operationalize ML workflows and ensure platform readiness for AI workloads
[...]
## Minimum Qualifications / Required Experience
[...]
* 8+ years of software engineering experience
[...]
* 4+ years of hands-on Kubernetes production experience with control plane ownership
* Strong experience operating on-prem or self-managed Kubernetes environments
[...]
* Strong proficiency in Go with experience building Kubernetes controllers, operators, CRDs, and webhooks
[...]
* Deep understanding of Kubernetes internals (API server, scheduler, controller loops, reconciliation)
* Experience supporting AI/ML or GPU-based workloads on Kubernetes platforms
[...]
## Preferred Qualifications
[...]
* Experience with bare-metal or enterprise on-prem infrastructure at scale
* Exposure to AI/ML platforms and tooling (e.g., Kubeflow, MLflow, distributed training systems)
* Experience building internal developer platforms or platform-as-a-service (PaaS) systems
* Familiarity with AIOps concepts such as automated remediation and predictive operations
* Experience applying data-driven or ML-based techniques for system reliability or capacity planning
[...]
* Contributions to Kubernetes, CNCF, or other open-source ecosystems
[...]
### Senior Kubernetes Platform Engineer - AI/ML Infrastructure
[...]
Cisco Systems
[...]
### Kubernetes Platform Engineer - AI Infrastructure
[...]
Cisco Systems
[...]
### Kubernetes Platform Engineer – AI Infrastructure

highlights:

# Kubernetes Platform Engineer – AI Infrastructure
3 weeks agoSan Jose, CaliforniaDurham, North CarolinahybridSeniorStaffDevOps & InfraML / AI
[...]
Lead design and operation of large-scale on-prem Kubernetes infrastructure for AI/ML workloads at Cisco, with ownership of control plane, GPU environments, and platform capabilities through custom controllers and operators.
[...]
Kubernetes Platform Engineer – AI Infrastructure - hybrid (2013055)
\*\*\*hybrid role - requires some work activity to be on-site in San Jose CA office
[...]
Join our Platform Engineering team to design, build, and operate large-scale, on-prem Kubernetes infrastructure powering next-generation AI/ML platforms, including GPU-enabled environments for traditional models and LLMs. You will lead the technical direction of scalable, reliable systems, managing the Kubernetes control plane and extending platform capabilities through custom controllers and operators. You’ll architect ML platforms, implement Infrastructure as Code with Golang, and drive MLOps best practices. Partnering closely with data scientists and ML engineers, you’ll enable high-performance AI workloads while leveraging AIOps for automation and reliability. highlights:
Software Engineer Data/AI/Intelligent Systems I (Full Time) – United States | Wfhforgeon

Wfhforgeon.← All Jobs

# Software Engineer Data/AI/Intelligent Systems I (Full Time) – United States

Posted May 28, 2026

Cisco is a leading technology company focused on revolutionizing how data and infrastructure connect in the AI era. They are seeking a Software Engineer specializing in Data, AI, and Intelligent Systems to develop scalable data pipelines, design analytics platforms, and support machine learning initiatives.

### Responsibilities

- Develop scalable data pipelines and analytics platforms
- Build infrastructure to support machine learning initiatives
- Ensure high-quality, reliable data infrastructure

Skills

- Completion within the past 3 years, or current enrollment with expected completion within 12 months, of a certification or relevant degree program (e.g., Associate's, Apprenticeship, Boot Camp, or Certification in a specialized program + 1 year of relevant experience, High School Diploma + 2 years of relevant experience) or Bachelor's + 0 years of relevant experience
- Proficiency in Python, with a strong understanding of fundamental data structures
- Hands-on experience with machine learning frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, or scikit-learn
- Familiarity with distributed data processing technologies, including Apache Spark, Hadoop (MapReduce), or Apache Flink
- Experience with containerization and orchestration tools, such as Docker and Kubernetes, for deploying data and machine learning workloads
- Hands-on experience with AI/ML
- Familiarity with major cloud platforms, such as AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud
- Understanding of distributed systems concepts, including scalability, reliability, fault tolerance, and data consistency, as well as familiarity with distributed computing patterns (e.g., load balancing, consensus algorithms, and inter-service communication)

### Benefits

- Medical, dental and vision insurance
- A 401(k) plan with a Cisco matching contribution
- Paid parental leave
- Short and long-term disability coverage
- Basic life insurance
- 10 paid holidays per full calendar year
- 1 floating holiday for non-exempt employees
- 1 paid day off for employee’s birthday
- Paid year-end holiday shutdown
- 4 paid days off for personal wellness determined by Cisco
- 16 days of paid vacation time per full calendar year
- Flexible vacation time off program
- 80 hours of sick time off provided on hire date and each January 1st thereafter
- Up to 80 hours of unused sick time carried forward from one calendar year to the next
- Additional paid time away may be requested to deal with critical or emergency issues for family members
- Optional 10 paid days per full calendar year to volunteer
- Employees are also eligible to earn annual bonuses subject to Cisco’s policies

Company Overview

- Cisco develops, manufactures, and sells networking hardware, telecommunications equipment, and other technology services and products. It is a sub-organization of Cisco Press. It was founded in 1984, and is headquartered in San Jose, California, USA, with a workforce of 10001+ employees. Its website is http://www.cisco.com. Company H1B Sponsorship

- Cisco has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 154 in 2026, 1238 in 2025, 1231 in 2024, 1273 in 2023, 2127 in 2022, 1991 in 2021, 1173 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

Apply for This Job

highlights:
The application window is expected to close on: 07/31/2026Job posting may be removed earlier if the position is filled or if a sufficient number of applications are received.This is a hybrid position located in Milpitas, CA. Ideally 3 days per week in office. Meet the TeamCisco is building a next-generation DPU-enabled networking platform for secure, high-performance infrastructure and large-scale AI clusters. Our team is responsible for designing the foundational networking, security, and programmable services that span host systems, DPUs, SmartNICs, and cloud-scale control planes. We work at the intersection of systems software, hardware acceleration, distributed security, and AI infrastructure, creating platforms that deliver line-rate performance, strong isolation, operational reliability, and simplified deployment across modern data center environments.We are looking for a senior software engineer to develop programmable packet processing pipelines and DPU SDK integrations across modern SmartNIC/DPU platforms, including NVIDIA BlueField, AMD Pensando, and similar architectures .This role focuses on the lower layers of the DPU networking stack: P4 or P4-like pipeline development, vendor SDKs, packet classification, policy enforcement, host networking offload, telemetry, and performance validation. The ideal candidate has strong systems programming skills, deep networking fundamentals, and hands-on experience building or integrating hardware-accelerated data paths.Your Impact

results.title: Agent Builder in Cloud Control Studio: A new way to extend and customize Cisco Cloud Control - Cisco Blogs
highlights:
##### Artificial Intelligence - AI
[...]
Today at Cisco Live, we are announcing Agent Builder in Cisco Cloud Control Studio, a new way to customize and extend Cisco Cloud Control with AI agents. Agent Builder is a key capability for making agents work the way teams operate.
[...]
Agent Builder is where teams bring their tools, knowledge, and operational expertise into Cisco Cloud Control. It brings together three of the most important parts of building agentic operations: connecting third-party products, building AI agents, and creating reusable agent skills. Builders describe what an agent should do in natural language, connect the tools and knowledge it needs, test it in a sandbox, and publish it through a versioned lifecycle.
[...]
Agent Builder is one of the capabilities being announced inside Cisco Cloud Control Studio, the new part of Cisco Cloud Control built for customizing and extending the platform with custom agents.
[...]
That’s why we unveiled Agent Builder, with more than 50 ecosystem integrations planned, so you can connect those systems directly to your agents. Native integrations are pre-built, validated, and configured through a guided flow. No custom code required. For systems without a native integration, Agent Builder supports Model Context Protocol (MCP), an open standard that lets AI agents securely access tools, data, and enterprise systems beyond Cisco. In the future, customers and partners will be able to build their own MCP connectors for any tool with an API.
[...]
Build AI agents that fit the work your teams do every day, including agents for compliance audits, morning health checks, incident triage, configuration drift monitoring, and more.
[...]
Ambient agents operate continuously in Cisco Cloud Control. They monitor data, watch conditions, and act or alert when something happens. An ambient compliance agent, for example, checks device configurations against company policy on a daily schedule and generates a report. Operators do not need to be in a session. The agent works in the background and notifies the team when it completes or needs approval.
[...]
Live agents are invoked during sessions in Cisco AI Canvas. They join active investigations alongside operators in real time, pulling context, correlating data, and proposing next steps as the work unfolds.
[...]
Agent Builder turns runbooks, SOPs, and procedures into reusable skills that agents can call when relevant. A skill might be a DNS troubleshooting procedure, a firewall validation step, or a compliance review checklist.
[...]
- Governance. Every agent runs under policy enforcement through Cisco AI Defense. Customers define what each agent can do, when it can act, and what actions require human approval. Agents propose. Humans approve before anything changes in production.
[...]
- Observability. Splunk AI Agent Monitoring captures every agent’s execution, including outcome, duration, trigger, and a full trace of every LLM call, tool invocation, and skill execution. Operators can drill into exactly what happened and why.
[...]
- Evaluation. Splunk AI Agent Monitoring, powered by Galileo, scores agent outputs for accuracy, relevance, and correctness. It flags hallucinations and detects drift when agent quality degrades over time.
[...]
- Lifecycle. Agents go through a draft, sandbox, and publish lifecycle. Builders verify behavior against real data before agents touch production. Version control tracks every change, with full rollback support.
[...]
Agent Builder in Cisco Cloud Control Studio is being announced at Cisco Live. If you are at the conference, come see it at the booth or in sessions throughout the week.
[...]
Tags: AI Security Artificial Intelligence (AI)
